rulings.Case Title: DORAN CONSTRUCTION & DESIGN INC. VS. ROBERT POYNTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Complainants Robert And Nili Poynter'S Notice Of Motion And Motion Of For Leave To Amend Answer And Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 1, 2021 line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT ROBERT POYNTER, NILI POYNTER Cross-Complainants Robert And Nili Poynter'S Notice Of Motion And Motion Of For Leave To Amend Answer And Cross-Complaint is GRANTED, subject to Cross-Defendant obtaining a trial continuance of no less than 3 months, with discovery remaining open in accordance with the new trial date. Cross-Complainant shall file and serve its motion to continue trial (set in department 206) no later than November 2, 2021. If motion to continue trial is granted Cross-Complainant shall file its amended complaint within 2 business days of the ruling granting trial continuance. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VOZIKES 2017 LLC VS. BRANDON CAMPBELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 1, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT BRANDON CAMPBELL MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is continued to November 5, 2021. By no later than 9:30 a.m. on November 1, 2021 Defendant to file a supplemental brief limited to the following: (1) cases standing for the proposition that employees of a corporation are parties to the action to which the corporation is a party; (2) cases discussing propriety/impropriety of service of summons and complaint by an employee; (3) cases holding that service by an employee is void under CCP 473(d) or any other statute/case law; (4) legislative history of the statutes governing service of summons and complaint on the issue of "party" definition; (5) cases holding that summons and complaint cannot be served on a tenant by a non-registered process server and must be served by a "marshal, sheriff or a registered process server." CCP 128.5. Brief shall not exceed 15 pages and must include table of contents and table of authorities. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
